title: Fields 2: Magnetic Fields and Induction
description: Magnetic flux density and field patterns, the forces on currents and moving charges, electromagnetic induction via Faraday's and Lenz's laws, and how generators and transformers put induction to work.
objectives: Describe the magnetic field patterns of a bar magnet, a solenoid and a straight current-carrying wire
Calculate the force on a current-carrying conductor using F = BIL sin(theta) and Fleming's left-hand rule
Calculate the force on a moving charge using F = BQv sin(theta) and describe the resulting circular motion
Define magnetic flux and flux linkage and calculate them for a coil in a uniform field
Apply Faraday's law and Lenz's law to find the magnitude and direction of an induced EMF
Explain the operation of a simple AC generator and calculate transformer voltages and currents
